design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Ren and Stimpy characters from the beloved animated series, experiencing the chaotic misadventures of the duo in platformer form
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed platformer that translates the show's surreal, comedic, and absurdist humor into gameplay; character-driven design leveraging the show's visual style and personality rather than innovative mechanics

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select accessible via cheat code (L, R, A, L, R, L, B, R, X, A at title screen) suggests developer-friendly progression bypass, possible difficulty tuning for varied audiences

progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden level select enables non-linear access to game's levels via cheat input
replayability_hooks: Character switching between Ren and Stimpy
Hidden level select for alternate progression paths
Potential thematic variety across level design (show-inspired settings)

world_building_techniques: Licensed IP integration—translates Ren & Stimpy Show aesthetic and character personalities into game world
Episode-like level design implied by character roles variety in source show (space explorers, Old West thieves, nature show hosts)

environmental_storytelling: Ren character archetype: emotionally unstable, psychotic chihuahua—volatile, dangerous personality
Stimpy character archetype: dimwitted, good-natured Manx cat—comedic, innocent counterpoint
Show's dark humor, shock value, and adult themes inform tone despite being platformer for console audience

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Ren & Stimpy Show: Veediots! (SNES)
Wikipedia - The Ren & Stimpy Show (character descriptions, show themes, and production context)
